import json, re, os, textwrap, sys, unicodedata, itertools, math, difflib, statistics
path_quran = "/mnt/data/file-Kjf8kioBqNjsW3Z6BUAgeu-quranic_verses.json"
path_hadith = "/mnt/data/file-D3aKALkQAv6nYj9sKob7Ho-six_hadith_books.json"

def load_json(path):
    with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        return json.load(f)
quran = load_json(path_quran)
len(quran), type(quran)

def normalize(s):
    # remove tashkeel and punctuation for matching
    # unify hamza/h alef forms?
    # For simple search, we can strip harakat
    import re, unicodedata
    s = str(s)
    # remove tashkeel
    tashkeel = ''.join(chr(i) for i in range(0x0610, 0x061A+1)) + ''.join(chr(i) for i in range(0x064B, 0x065F+1)) + ''.join(chr(i) for i in range(0x0670, 0x0670+1)) + ''.join(chr(i) for i in range(0x06D6, 0x06ED+1))
    s = re.sub(f"[{re.escape(tashkeel)}]", "", s)
    # tatweel
    s = s.replace("ـ","")
    # unify alef forms
    s = re.sub("[إأٱآا]", "ا", s)
    s = re.sub("ى", "ي", s)
    s = re.sub("ؤ", "و", s)
    s = re.sub("ئ", "ي", s)
    s = re.sub("ٰ", "", s) # dagger alef
    # remove punctuation and brackets
    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", s, flags=re.UNICODE)
    # collapse spaces
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

snippet = "وَالَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ"
norm_snippet = normalize(snippet)
norm_snippet[:200]

# Search for verses containing key words
matches = []
for v in quran:
    text = v['ayah_text']
    if "يَبْخَلُونَ" in text and "يَأْمُرُونَ" in text and "مَا آتَاهُمُ" in text:
        matches.append(v)
len(matches), matches[:2]

candidate = matches[0]['ayah_text']
def strip_full(s):
    # unify as earlier
    return normalize(s)

from difflib import SequenceMatcher
ratio = SequenceMatcher(a=strip_full(snippet), b=strip_full(candidate)).ratio()
strip_full(snippet), strip_full(candidate), ratio

def lcs_length(a,b):
    # compute lcs length dynamic programming for manageable length
    n, m = len(a), len(b)
    # Use dp with 1D
    dp = [0]*(m+1)
    for i in range(1, n+1):
        prev = 0
        for j in range(1, m+1):
            tmp = dp[j]
            if a[i-1]==b[j-1]:
                dp[j] = prev + 1
            else:
                dp[j] = max(dp[j], dp[j-1])
            prev = tmp
    return dp[m]

ns = strip_full(snippet)
nc = strip_full(candidate)
lcs = lcs_length(ns, nc)
lcs, len(ns), len(nc), lcs/len(ns), lcs/max(len(ns), len(nc)), lcs/min(len(ns), len(nc))
